title: The Role of Sports Psychology
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Athletes at all levels are discovering the benefits of a (0) psychological approach to training and competition. The ability to manage nerves and stay calm under pressure can dramatically improve sporting (17) performance. Building genuine (18) confidence before a major event is just as important as physical preparation. Many champions agree that a (19) successful mindset is what separates the best from the rest. Setting clear goals helps athletes measure their (20) achievements and stay focused over time. Sports psychologists teach athletes to deal (21) mentally with the pressure of competition, turning negative thoughts into positive ones. An athlete who is (22) unfocused during a competition is unlikely to perform at their best level. Maintaining (23) motivation throughout a long season requires planning, rest, and strong self-belief. Intensive (24) training without psychological support can lead to burnout, leaving athletes unable to enjoy their sport.

example.text: Athletes at all levels are discovering the benefits of a (0) PSYCHOLOGICAL approach to training and competition. (PSYCHOLOGY)

questions.explanation: A noun is needed before 'sporting'; 'perform' becomes 'performance' by adding the suffix '-ance'.
A noun is needed after 'genuine'; 'confident' becomes 'confidence' by changing '-dent' to '-dence'.
An adjective is needed before 'mindset'; 'success' becomes 'successful' by adding the suffix '-ful'.
A plural noun is needed after 'their'; 'achieve' becomes 'achievement' with '-ment', then pluralised.
An adverb is needed to modify 'deal'; 'mental' becomes 'mentally' by adding the suffix '-ly'.
A negative adjective is needed; the prefix 'un-' is added to 'focused' (from 'focus') to mean 'lacking concentration'.
A noun is needed after 'Maintaining'; 'motivate' becomes 'motivation' by replacing '-ate' with '-ation'.
A noun is needed before 'without'; 'train' becomes 'training' by adding the suffix '-ing'.
